refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the mechanisms that enable it, and acknowledges that both on-site and tele-operated modes must be supported. The constraint on safety compliance is explicit.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over the agreed evaluation period; measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) against the customer-defined target; audit compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies via safety logs and incident reports.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed, precision, and cycle efficiency to achieve the productivity target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local cab or remote tele-operation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, productive task execution.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report task progress, equipment state, and safety-critical parameters via telemetry to enable real-time productivity tracking and safety oversight.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety interlocks and alerts to prevent unsafe operations that would violate the worksite safety plan.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance hydraulic earthmoving vehicle with optimized bucket geometry, engine power, and cycle time; couple with a low-latency tele-operation system and real-time telemetry pipeline to enable both local and remote operation with continuous productivity and safety monitoring.

DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with configurable attachments and operator modes (local/remote); implement a unified telemetry and tasking protocol that abstracts the operator interface from the vehicle control layer, allowing independent optimization of each.

DP-C: Implement a productivity-aware task scheduler and vehicle controller that dynamically adjusts operating parameters (engine throttle, hydraulic flow, cycle timing) based on real-time telemetry feedback and worksite conditions to maximize throughput while respecting safety constraints.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with configurable attachments and operator modes (local/remote); implement a unified telemetry and tasking protocol that abstracts the operator interface from the vehicle control layer, allowing independent optimization of each.

potential_sources: Coupling between vehicle mechanical design and tele-operation latency requirements: mitigated by explicit protocol abstraction (Tasking, Telemetry interfaces).
Coupling between productivity optimization and safety enforcement: mitigated by safety services layer that operates independently on telemetry stream.
Coupling between local and remote operator modes: mitigated by unified control interface (Tasking protocol) that decouples operator input from vehicle actuation.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B achieves low coupling by introducing explicit protocol boundaries (Tasking, Telemetry) that allow vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to evolve independently. This supports both productivity and safety concerns without tight interdependence.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Unified Tasking protocol can be designed with sufficient expressiveness to support both local and remote operator modes without performance degradation.
Assumption: Real-time telemetry pipeline can deliver productivity and safety-critical data with latency ≤ acceptable threshold for remote operation.
Risk: If Tasking protocol is too abstract, local operator may lack fine-grained control needed for complex earthmoving tasks.
Risk: If telemetry latency exceeds tele-operator reaction time, remote operation may become unsafe or inefficient.
Risk: Modular vehicle platform may introduce mechanical complexity or cost that conflicts with productivity target.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Proceed with DP-B but establish explicit performance contracts for Tasking protocol latency and Telemetry data rate early in design. Conduct human-factors analysis to validate that unified control interface supports both local and remote operator cognitive load.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It owns the Tasking and Telemetry protocol definitions, the integration test harness, and the overall productivity and safety KPI measurement framework. It does not own the detailed mechanical or software implementation of any subordinate module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator (local or remote) sends task commands (dig, load, transport, dump, stop) to the NGHE Solution. Tasking protocol abstracts operator mode and is consumed by Vehicle and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits real-time telemetry stream containing vehicle state (position, bucket load, engine RPM, hydraulic pressure), task progress (cycle count, material moved), and safety-critical parameters (proximity alerts, operator input, safety interlocks). Consumed by Site Manager, Tele-operator, Safety Manager, and internal Safety Services module.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits high-priority safety alerts (imminent collision, operator override, safety interlock activation) to Safety Manager and Tele-operator. Triggers immediate intervention or task abort.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for remote tele-operation, telemetry uplink, and safety alert distribution. Owned by Teleoperation module but exposed at NGHE Solution boundary for network infrastructure integration.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for CR-NGHE-001. It owns the productivity KPI measurement, the integration of Vehicle + Teleoperation + Safety Services, and the protocol definitions (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert). Minimal diffusion: only Network Link is partially owned by Teleoperation module, but NGHE Solution exposes it as a system-level interface.

Vehicle module owns the mechanical and hydraulic systems that execute tasks and generate productivity. However, CR-NGHE-001 requires integration of operator interface (local/remote), telemetry, and safety services—all of which span beyond Vehicle. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for productivity KPI across multiple modules.

Teleoperation module owns the remote operator interface and network link. However, CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level productivity KPI that depends on vehicle performance, local operator capability, and safety compliance—all outside Teleoperation scope. Assigning to Teleoperation would create false ownership of vehicle productivity.

Safety Services module owns safety monitoring and interlock enforcement. However, CR-NGHE-001 is primarily a productivity KPI with safety as a constraint, not the primary objective. Assigning to Safety Services would misalign ownership with the primary functional goal.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI) is a system-level requirement that cannot be satisfied by any single subordinate module. It requires coordinated execution across Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (safety compliance). The NGHE Solution module is the natural owner because it integrates these three modules, defines the Tasking and Telemetry protocols that coordinate them, and owns the measurement framework for the productivity KPI itself.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution ownership may be perceived as too abstract or high-level, lacking concrete implementation responsibility. Mitigation: Establish clear ownership of protocol definitions (Tasking, Telemetry) and integration test harness within NGHE Solution; allocate sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with explicit traceability to CR-NGHE-001.

Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules are not yet defined or have unclear boundaries, NGHE Solution ownership becomes ambiguous. Mitigation: Confirm that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are well-defined modules with clear interfaces before finalizing NGHE Solution ownership.

Risk: Productivity measurement may require instrumentation or data processing that is not owned by any subordinate module. Mitigation: Allocate productivity measurement logic (data collection, KPI calculation, reporting) explicitly to NGHE Solution or a designated sub-module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution with explicit sub-FR allocation to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation (FR-NGHE-001.2), Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.4), and a new Instrumentation/Telemetry module (FR-NGHE-001.3). Establish clear ownership of Tasking and Telemetry protocol definitions within NGHE Solution. Conduct interface review to confirm that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules can be integrated via these protocols without hidden dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 and sub-FRs are clearly mapped to DP-B (modular vehicle platform with unified Tasking/Telemetry protocols). DP-B directly addresses the need for both local and remote operation, productivity measurement, and safety compliance without over-constraining the design. The mapping is traceable and non-circular.

diffusion_risks: Tasking protocol definition is owned by NGHE Solution but must be implemented by Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Risk: Protocol changes could require rework in multiple modules. Mitigation: Establish protocol versioning and backward compatibility rules.

Telemetry pipeline spans Vehicle (data generation), Teleoperation (network transmission), and Safety Services (data consumption). Risk: Latency or data loss in any component could degrade productivity or safety. Mitigation: Define end-to-end telemetry SLA (latency, reliability) at NGHE Solution level; allocate sub-requirements to each module.

Productivity measurement requires data from Vehicle (task progress), Teleoperation (operator input), and Safety Services (safety events). Risk: Inconsistent data formats or timestamps could corrupt KPI calculation. Mitigation: Standardize telemetry data schema and time synchronization at NGHE Solution level.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment is strong for protocol ownership and integration responsibility. Diffusion is acceptable because it is managed via explicit interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Each subordinate module has clear responsibility for implementing its side of the protocol.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-001 verification is feasible via representative operations trials. Productivity (m³/h or ton/h) is directly measurable from telemetry data. Safety compliance is auditable via safety logs and incident reports. Operator interface quality (situational awareness, control responsiveness) is assessable via human-factors testing and operator feedback.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Sub-FRs are clearly allocated to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation (FR-NGHE-001.2), Instrumentation/Telemetry (FR-NGHE-001.3), and Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.4). Each sub-FR is traceable to a specific module and can be verified independently. Integration verification is performed at NGHE Solution level via end-to-end productivity and safety KPI measurement.

recommended_revisions: Establish explicit performance contracts for Tasking protocol latency (≤ X ms for local operation, ≤ Y ms for remote operation) and Telemetry data rate (≥ Z Hz for safety-critical parameters). These contracts must be defined at NGHE Solution level and allocated to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

Define a detailed telemetry data schema (message format, field definitions, timestamp synchronization) at NGHE Solution level. This schema must be implemented consistently by Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to ensure reliable productivity and safety KPI calculation.

Conduct human-factors analysis to validate that the unified Tasking protocol (DP-B) provides sufficient operator control and situational awareness for both local and remote modes. If analysis reveals gaps, revise DP-B to include mode-specific control extensions.

Establish a productivity measurement algorithm (data collection, filtering, KPI calculation) at NGHE Solution level. Specify how productivity is calculated from telemetry data (e.g., cycle count × bucket volume, or direct load cell measurement). Allocate instrumentation requirements to Vehicle module.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system KPI that cannot be satisfied by a single design parameter or module. Refinement has successfully decomposed it into four atomic sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4), each of which is assignable to a specific module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Instrumentation/Telemetry, Safety Services) and verifiable via concrete tests. The selected DP-B (modular platform with unified protocols) is sound and supports independent evolution of each sub-system. NGHE Solution is the correct owner at this level. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will refine each sub-FR into detailed design parameters and module responsibilities.
decision.justification_detail: The gate review confirms that FR-DP-IM alignment is strong. No fundamental contradictions or coupling issues were identified. The recommended revisions (protocol contracts, telemetry schema, human-factors analysis) are design-level details that should be addressed during the next decomposition level, not at this level. Proceeding to ZIGZAG_DOWN allows the design team to focus on each sub-FR independently while maintaining traceability to the top-level productivity KPI.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed, precision, and cycle efficiency to achieve the productivity target. [Assigned to: Vehicle module]

FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local cab or remote tele-operation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, productive task execution. [Assigned to: Teleoperation module]

FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report task progress, equipment state, and safety-critical parameters via telemetry to enable real-time productivity tracking and safety oversight. [Assigned to: Instrumentation/Telemetry module (new) or Vehicle module with Teleoperation support]
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety interlocks and alerts to prevent unsafe operations that would violate the worksite safety plan. [Assigned to: Safety Services module]

assumptions: Assumption-1: The customer-defined productivity target (m³/h or ton/h) is known and achievable with current heavy equipment technology.
Assumption-2: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are documented and available for review during design and verification.
Assumption-3: A unified Tasking protocol can be designed with sufficient expressiveness to support both local and remote operator modes without performance degradation.
Assumption-4: Real-time telemetry pipeline can deliver productivity and safety-critical data with latency ≤ acceptable threshold for remote operation (to be defined in next level).
Assumption-5: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules are well-defined with clear boundaries and interfaces.
Assumption-6: Productivity measurement can be performed via telemetry data (e.g., cycle count, load cell readings) without requiring external instrumentation.
Assumption-7: Safety criticality (high) does not require formal certification (e.g., ISO 26262, IEC 61508) at this stage; safety requirements will be refined in next level.
open_risks: Risk-1: If Tasking protocol is too abstract, local operator may lack fine-grained control needed for complex earthmoving tasks. Mitigation: Conduct human-factors analysis in next level.
Risk-2: If telemetry latency exceeds tele-operator reaction time, remote operation may become unsafe or inefficient. Mitigation: Establish latency SLA in next level; conduct remote operation trials.

Risk-3: Modular vehicle platform may introduce mechanical complexity or cost that conflicts with productivity target. Mitigation: Conduct cost-benefit analysis in next level; compare modular vs. integrated designs.

Risk-4: Productivity measurement algorithm may be corrupted by inconsistent data formats or timestamps across modules. Mitigation: Standardize telemetry data schema at NGHE Solution level in next level.

Risk-5: Safety Services module may not have sufficient visibility into operator intent (via Tasking protocol) to prevent unsafe operations. Mitigation: Define safety-critical Tasking protocol fields in next level; conduct safety analysis.

Risk-6: Integration of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules may reveal hidden dependencies or interface mismatches. Mitigation: Conduct interface review and integration testing in next level.
evidence_needed: Evidence-1: Customer-defined productivity target (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period.
Evidence-2: Worksite safety plan and operational policies documentation.
Evidence-3: Tasking protocol specification (message format, command set, latency requirements).
Evidence-4: Telemetry data schema (field definitions, sampling rate, timestamp synchronization).
Evidence-5: Vehicle module specification (mechanical design, hydraulic system, control interface).
Evidence-6: Teleoperation module specification (remote operator interface, network link, latency budget).
Evidence-7: Safety Services module specification (safety monitoring, interlock logic, alert generation).
Evidence-8: Instrumentation specification (sensors, data acquisition, productivity calculation algorithm).
Evidence-9: Human-factors analysis report (operator control, situational awareness, cognitive load).
Evidence-10: Integration test plan and results (end-to-end productivity and safety KPI verification).
Evidence-11: Representative operations trial results (productivity, safety, operator feedback).
